== Notes for [[Item:Q90136|Q90136]] ==

From Wikipedia:

'''[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Titus_Flavius_Sabinus_(consul_69&#41 Titus Flavius Sabinus]''' was the son of [http://www.geni.com/people/Titus-Flavius-Sabinus/6000000013204919137 Titus Flavius Sabinus] (consul 47). He was suffect consul in May and June 69 and one of the generals who fought for Otho against Vitellius during the Year of the Four Emperors, although he submitted to Vitellius once Otho had been defeated. He was besieged alongside his father in the Capital, but escaped when it was burnt down. His paternal cousin, Emperor [http://www.geni.com/people/Domitian-Roman-Emperor/6000000008059993700 Domitian], later had him put to death after a herald saluted him as imperator ("commander", a title only the emperor was entitled to) instead of "consul".

He was the father of [http://www.geni.com/people/Titus-Flavius-Sabinus/6000000005379570295 Titus Flavius Sabinus] and [http://www.geni.com/people/Titus-Flavius-Clemens/6000000009144745200 Titus Flavius Clemens].

== Sources ==

* Tacitus, ''Histories''
* Suetonius, ''Lives of the Twelve Caesars''
* Cassius Dio, ''Roman History''
* Arnold Blumberg (ed) (1995), ''Great Leaders, Great Tyrants?: Contemporary Views of World Rulers Who Made History''

----------------------------------------------------

ID: I66434

Name: Publius Falvius Sabinus

Given Name: Publius Falvius

Surname: Sabinus

Sex: M

_UID: 9AB4C70F29C7E34DB54829AE51C11EFA4296

Change Date: 3 Oct 2005

Death: deceased

Father: Titus Flavius II Sabinus

Marriage 1 Spouse Unknown

Children

Titus Flavius Sabinus

Forrás / Source:

http://wc.rootsweb.ancestry.com/cgi-bin/igm.cgi?op=GET&db=jdp-fam&id=I66434

--------------------

Reference: http://fabpedigree.com/s040/f000134.htm

--------------------

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Titus_Flavius_Sabinus_(consul_69)
